result.summary: Found several visual layout issues, mostly related to content being clipped at the right edge and the fixed bottom navigation overlapping the game grid.

issues.description: Multiple horizontal sections appear clipped on the right side of the viewport. The top category row cuts off the “Hot Games” tab, and the game rows show partially visible cards on the right edge.
issues.suggestion: Review the mobile responsive layout. If these are intended to be horizontal carousels, add clearer scroll affordance and consistent card spacing. If not, constrain content to the viewport and use a proper 2-column mobile grid.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the game content near the bottom of the screen, hiding part of the lower game cards.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the page content so scrollable content is not covered by the fixed footer navigation.

issues.description: The hero carousel content and right-side arrow are partially cut off at the right edge, making the banner feel cropped or misaligned.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the carousel slide width matches the viewport and keep navigation arrows fully inside the visible area.

issues.description: The selected “Home” category pill has an odd blue/orange shadow or gradient smear underneath it, which looks like a rendering or overflow artifact.
issues.suggestion: Check the active tab shadow/gradient styling and clip or remove any unintended overflow effects.

issues.description: Some game card overlays, such as jackpot amounts and info icons, are close to the card edges and appear cramped, especially on clipped cards.
issues.suggestion: Add consistent internal padding and verify overlay positioning across responsive breakpoints.
